task.description: Search paginated users and count admins across all pages
task.prompt: Use the search_users tool to fetch all pages of users (start at page 1, continue until you've fetched all pages based on the total_pages field). Find all users with role "admin" across all pages. Print the count of admin users and their usernames, one per line.

tools.name: search_users
tools.description: Search users with pagination. Returns a JSON object with page, total_pages, and data (array of user objects with fields: id, username, role).

content.text: Use the search_users tool to fetch all pages of users (start at page 1, continue until you've fetched all pages based on the total_pages field). Find all users with role "admin" across all pages. Print the count of admin users and their usernames, one per line.
I'll help you fetch all pages of users and find the admin users. Let me start by getting the first page to see the total number of pages, then fetch all pages and filter for admin users.

input.commands:
set -e

# First, get page 1 to see total_pages
echo "Fetching page 1 to determine total pages..."
page1_result=$(search_users --page 1)
echo "$page1_result" | jq '.'

# Extract total_pages
total_pages=$(echo "$page1_result" | jq -r '.total_pages')
echo "Total pages: $total_pages"

# Initialize array to collect all admin users
admin_users=()

# Fetch all pages and collect admin users
for ((page=1; page<=total_pages; page++)); do
    echo "Fetching page $page..."
    result=$(search_users --page "$page")
    
    # Extract admin users from this page
    page_admins=$(echo "$result" | jq -r '.data[] | select(.role == "admin") | .username')
    
    # Add to our collection
    if [ -n "$page_admins" ]; then
        while IFS= read -r username; do
            admin_users+=("$username")
        done <<< "$page_admins"
    fi
done

# Print results
echo ""
echo "Admin users found: ${#admin_users[@]}"
echo ""
echo "Admin usernames:"
for username in "${admin_users[@]}"; do
    echo "$username"
done

content.text:
Perfect! I've successfully fetched all 3 pages of users and found the admin users. Here are the results:

**Admin users found: 2**

**Admin usernames:**
- alice
- leo

The search traversed all 3 pages of user data and identified 2 users with the "admin" role across the entire dataset.

task.description: Aggregate ERROR log entries across paginated log pages
task.prompt: Use the get_logs tool to fetch all pages of log entries (start at page 1, continue until you've fetched all pages using total_pages). Count all entries where level is "ERROR" and list their timestamps. Print each ERROR timestamp on its own line, then print the total count of ERROR entries.

tools.name: get_logs
tools.description: Fetch paginated log entries. Returns JSON with page, total_pages, and data (array of log objects with fields: timestamp, level, message, service).

content.text: Use the get_logs tool to fetch all pages of log entries (start at page 1, continue until you've fetched all pages using total_pages). Count all entries where level is "ERROR" and list their timestamps. Print each ERROR timestamp on its own line, then print the total count of ERROR entries.
I'll fetch all pages of log entries and count the ERROR level entries. Let me start by getting the first page to see the structure and total pages.
Now I'll fetch all 4 pages and collect all ERROR entries:

input.commands:
set -e

# Initialize arrays to store all error timestamps
error_timestamps=()

# Fetch all pages (1 through 4)
for page in {1..4}; do
    echo "Fetching page $page..." >&2
    page_data=$(get_logs --page $page)
    
    # Extract ERROR timestamps from this page
    error_ts=$(echo "$page_data" | jq -r '.data[] | select(.level == "ERROR") | .timestamp')
    
    # Add to our collection
    if [[ -n "$error_ts" ]]; then
        while IFS= read -r timestamp; do
            error_timestamps+=("$timestamp")
        done <<< "$error_ts"
    fi
done

# Print each ERROR timestamp on its own line
echo "ERROR timestamps:"
for ts in "${error_timestamps[@]}"; do
    echo "$ts"
done

# Print total count
echo ""
echo "Total ERROR entries: ${#error_timestamps[@]}"

content.content:
ERROR timestamps:
2024-03-15T08:01:12Z
2024-03-15T08:04:59Z
2024-03-15T09:03:48Z
2024-03-15T10:04:58Z
2024-03-15T11:01:22Z

Total ERROR entries: 5

STDERR: Fetching page 1...
Fetching page 2...
Fetching page 3...
Fetching page 4...

content.text:
Perfect! I've successfully fetched all 4 pages of log entries and found all ERROR level entries. Here are the results:

**ERROR timestamps:**
2024-03-15T08:01:12Z
2024-03-15T08:04:59Z
2024-03-15T09:03:48Z
2024-03-15T10:04:58Z
2024-03-15T11:01:22Z

**Total ERROR entries: 5**

The script fetched all 4 pages of logs, filtered for entries with level "ERROR", and found 5 ERROR entries total across all pages.

task.description: Audit inventory across paginated products and identify out-of-stock items
task.prompt: Use the list_products tool to fetch all pages of products (start at page 1, continue until all pages fetched using total_pages). For each product, use the get_stock tool with the product's id to check its stock level. Identify all products where stock is 0 (out of stock). Print the names of out-of-stock products, one per line, and print the total count of out-of-stock items.

tools.name: list_products
tools.description: List products with pagination. Returns JSON with page, total_pages, and data (array of product objects with fields: id, name, category).

tools.name: get_stock
tools.description: Get stock level for a product by its ID. Returns JSON with product_id and stock fields.

product_id.description: The product ID to check stock for

content.text: Use the list_products tool to fetch all pages of products (start at page 1, continue until all pages fetched using total_pages). For each product, use the get_stock tool with the product's id to check its stock level. Identify all products where stock is 0 (out of stock). Print the names of out-of-stock products, one per line, and print the total count of out-of-stock items.

I'll help you fetch all products and check their stock levels to identify out-of-stock items. Let me start by getting the first page to see how many total pages there are, then fetch all products and check their stock levels.

input.commands:
set -e

# Start by getting the first page to see total pages
echo "Fetching first page to determine total pages..."
first_page=$(list_products --page 1)
total_pages=$(echo "$first_page" | jq -r '.total_pages')
echo "Total pages: $total_pages"

# Array to store all products
all_products="[]"

# Fetch all pages
for page in $(seq 1 $total_pages); do
    echo "Fetching page $page..."
    page_data=$(list_products --page $page)
    products=$(echo "$page_data" | jq -r '.data')
    all_products=$(echo "$all_products" | jq ". + $products")
done

echo "Total products fetched: $(echo "$all_products" | jq length)"

# Check stock for each product and identify out-of-stock items
out_of_stock_products="[]"
out_of_stock_count=0

echo "Checking stock levels..."
for product_id in $(echo "$all_products" | jq -r '.[].id'); do
    stock_data=$(get_stock --product_id "$product_id")
    stock_level=$(echo "$stock_data" | jq -r '.stock')
    
    if [ "$stock_level" -eq 0 ]; then
        product_name=$(echo "$all_products" | jq -r ".[] | select(.id == \"$product_id\") | .name")
        out_of_stock_products=$(echo "$out_of_stock_products" | jq ". + [\"$product_name\"]")
        out_of_stock_count=$((out_of_stock_count + 1))
    fi
done

echo ""
echo "=== OUT OF STOCK PRODUCTS ==="
if [ $out_of_stock_count -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "No products are out of stock."
else
    echo "$out_of_stock_products" | jq -r '.[]'
fi

echo ""
echo "Total out-of-stock items: $out_of_stock_count"

content.content:
Fetching first page to determine total pages...
Total pages: 3
Fetching page 1...
Fetching page 2...
Fetching page 3...
Total products fetched: 9
Checking stock levels...

=== OUT OF STOCK PRODUCTS ===
USB-C Cable
Monitor Stand
Laptop Sleeve

Total out-of-stock items: 3

content.text:
Perfect! I've successfully fetched all products across all 3 pages and checked the stock level for each one. Here are the results:

**Out-of-stock products:**
- USB-C Cable
- Monitor Stand  
- Laptop Sleeve

**Total out-of-stock items: 3**

The script fetched 9 total products from 3 pages and found that 3 of them have zero stock remaining.

# Start by getting the first page to see total pages

done
